key_items: Bitcoin rejected near $78,000 with bears reclaiming control; longest deleveraging since 2022 ending but options traders cautious
Multiple high-profile Bitcoin sales by Saylor/MicroStrategy (2026 exit plan), Mark Cuban (failed hedge), and Trump Media ($205M transfer) signal distribution pressure

Fidelity calls BTC an early bull market with tightest monthly Bollinger Bands ever, but Kalshi traders moved $100K odds from 40% to 0% YoY; regulatory headwinds (SEC tokenized stocks delay, insider trading probes) add uncertainty
reasoning: Bitcoin faces conflicting signals with technical setup (tight BBands, early bull signals) offset by significant selling pressure from mega-holders and institutional distribution. The rejection below $78K combined with zero conviction on $100K targets suggests consolidation rather than immediate upside momentum for swing traders in the 1-7 day window.
risks: Cascade of high-profile seller capitulation (Saylor, Cuban, Trump Media) could trigger forced liquidations if price breaks below $74-75K support, collapsing sentiment further and attracting retail panic selling.
